you only need it to happen once and it'll ruin you forever on what this living thing is all about, and thereafter it stalks your mind and like a conspiracy you start to see it in every possibility - that hair, that laugh, that sigh
you're haunted by the past and yearn for the future, and the present is anything but here, face smooshed against the window of what was, of what could be

sometimes i wish i never did it, but i have to remind myself that it is eros which pulls against the death drive - without the tension of desire we fall apart into nothingness 

and besides, it was never my choice anyway

tension is what holds us together - it's what holds it all together

to be tensionless is to resolve the ever morphing yin and yang into absolute stillness, utter release, the final breath out

and sometimes that's really nice, but to take the final breath out means there will never be another breath in, and there's at least a few more sunsets i hope to see before that happens, a few more lost nights of sleep and cuddles and screams and icecreams

i heard that the old and wise have figured this out, and i feel like i have a few tumbles to take before i realise that i ought to stop stepping on banana peels, or at least become Charlie Chaplin to make it a more delightful affair

so i guess i'll keep on stepping on banana peels till then

this was prompted by elaine's examples of acting with grace towards people who we have difficult relationships with

i'm tweeting as i'm reading - she draws a great link to Maslow's work, and i'll double-down on that link here for my purposes
when our needs aren't met, it's really, really hard to be loving and forgiving and all that Nice and Holy shtick that we strive to become
maslow's tippy top need is 'transcendence' - going beyond ourselves (doing things for reasons other than ourselves) - that's at the *end* of figuring out our other needs!

i mean, you could do it w/o the other needs! 

but i think it runs high risk of damaging the 'vessel' who's channeling this self-less work

not a direct example, but related (from the introduction, which isn't written by weil but is about her ideas): https://t.co/xYgCj8Qh1D
to be selfless because the self is full and thus empty is distinct from the self being vacant from having withered away
to be clear, there ought to be a continual dialogue between fulfilling needs of the self and working with grace for the benefit of others
but as we are not God and the lives of saints are not fit for most of us, having clear in sight the idea that "boundaries are the distance at which I can love you and me simultaneously" is crucial

i've been thinking about this insightful piece on conversation & reflecting on my own conversational disposition - how i show up in conversation and for what reason 

i'm curious to hear what your natural tendencies are, and if they switch!

https://t.co/Acrjcf8e2b https://t.co/qHeMZXJt1Y
the piece talks about "givers" and "takers" - givers think about conversations in terms of "invitations" while takers progress through "a series of declarations"
author makes a great case of why one isn't automatically better than the other, and it's moreso about the 'openings' (the 'doorknobs') we give rather than, e.g, just asking questions
i'm going to hone in on giver / taker idea and expand on that
for me, sometimes i love 'sermonising' and weaving together multiple ideas in succession, and sometimes i revel in the conversational presence of others
there's another dimension of this where i'll try to be a 'taker' because i'm feeling insecure and want to impress, or shut down into being a 'giver'
i think generally, when i'm comfortable, the kinds of conversation shifts my disposition, (late night convo vs casual lunch at work)

could frame this as depth of conversation

if it's a casual conversation and, e.g, people are relating funny anecdotes, i much prefer to sit back and enjoy laughing and supporting because i usually don't have anything to contribute, and that's ok!

in headier conversations, i become weighted towards 'taker' mode, although this definitely depends because often i really enjoy hearing what other people have to say too, and (esp. when comfortable) i'm more than happy to be asking questions 95% of the time
when i'm anxious, my closeness with who i'm with shifts disposition (friends where i have nothing to prove vs new group that i'm trying to fit into)
when i'm comfortable, i can easily flow between giver / taker depending on situation, when i'm anxious, i lock into one or the other
i'd love to hear how any of you relate to all this!

meditation lately has been about the gentlest of touches

when thoughts get caught in my mind's net, i tell them they're perfectly okay and give them little forehead kisses

usually that relaxes their struggle and they untangle themselves free
the "problem" of my monkey mind isn't that i give my thoughts too much attention, it's that i don't give them the good kind of *warm* attention

okayness

one of the fun things about being young and dumb is that i get to experience a lot of worldview upheavals 

i had wished that i would continue having them into old age but i think that misses the subtle sweetness that pervades the Good Life

https://t.co/JRKOvelgAm

the milestones are awesome, no doubt!! 

they're exciting and are the materials which weave the bed of personal myths 

the sharing of those myths is also what helps bind us more closely to one another

and! on top of that! attunement to the soft turnings of living is what blooms richer flavours 

it is what allows us to perceive further and further the fractal spark of that essential Beauty
it's the difference between electricity of first falling in love, and the decades of in-between moments afterwards that make the texture of that relationship

i'm not exactly saying to "live slower" 

living slower definitely helps develop that sensibility by putting us in an 'experiential environment' where that sort of sensitivity becomes more 'accident prone' to happen and is easier to sustain, but it's not strictly necessary
i'm also not saying "pay attention to the little things" - that makes it sound like all ways of attending are the same

rather, there's a way of relating with such little things (or other very big things) that can enrich over time so that every time you watch it, it twinkles a little more, and eventually attending to its magic becomes as easy as opening up the blinds to let in the warming sun

and so: grooving into an ever deepening, quiet joy with the clouds, with the ecstasy of dance, with that first hour after you wake

slowly, slowly - each thing becomes evermore full, evermore spacious - it's always at once the cherished familiar and, too, the swirling mystery

maybe one aspect the elder sage's wisdom is exactly the refinement of this touch with living itself 

to ceaselessly fall deeper in love with the world, moment by moment

ok so i have a Take on this great metaphor which is that, yes, both-and, but critically that for the Good Life, lightness *necessarily* grounds heaviness and is in fact the soil from which wholeness fruits 

this crucial relationship is what conceives aliveness https://t.co/OgR9jYQ5ix

the encapsulated version is in the QT'd reply below

the disclaimer is that i prob have different framing / definition of heaviness / lightness which will lead to object-level disagreements with even nicole herself, but i think it points to similar things

https://t.co/oLarl5yt42
lightness births fluidity - it can flow with the unknown, rest among paradox, sit at the very edge of unfathomable experience

heaviness is a piercing patterning of the "blooming, buzzing confusion" that is living through the lens of the mind - a locking together of systems, a distillation and clarification of essence in a symbolic form

here's the thing - heaviness is the thinkyboi's mode of being, and believe me i love thinkybois and their galaxy brains and on many levels aspire to be one, but you can think as deep as you want and will always remain ten steps removed from the Thing itself

i don't think we actually need to be a thinkyboi to live the Good Life! 

vibe-maxxing offers in itself an overfilling richness of what life has to offer - no mediation of the intellectual mind necessary!

and vibes are ruled by the domain of lightness

the nature of lightness unfolds our attempt to pin our self-worth to it, because if you groove deep enough with it, we realise that things like the interestingness of the self is an idea to be played with too, to throw around like a ball and balance on your nose with

and lest you believe lightness (play, silliness, humour) to be trivial, the humour of the heart and the freest of play is a matter of utmost seriousness - it comes not from ignorance of pain but from seeing its absolute necessity in such a marred world
the clowns who provoke the warmest laughs are those who have shed uncountable tears

heaviness is a wonderful companion to lightness - not only a way to make clearer sense of what this is all about, but also a way to relate with our fellow humans with colour that would not be possible without it
but, without lightness, heaviness collapses in under its own weight - the gravity of its abstractions swirls itself into the pits of its self-referential logic

heaviness sets on a stringent path to observe the stars through the telescope, lightness reminds it of its singular instrument, and suggests that signing a tune for each of the sparks it sees with its naked eye is also a way to honor and deeply know the twinkling above
heaviness from the view of lightness thrives, but lightness cannot be understood from the view of heaviness

i saw the importance of lightness through heaviness, and pursued it because of that, but it was only when i could continually shed of heaviness could i give myself to lightness and dance among its gardens
so heaviness is necessary for some to approach lightness (certainly was for me), but once lightness is befriended, heaviness is no longer needed for aliveness

as heaviness broaches unto the ineffable, it must loosen itself for it requires comfort with that which cannot be articulated

heaviness, with its discerning mind, runs against linguistic chaos
lightness holds the furrowed brows of heaviness, steeps in the textures of its agony, but it is lightness that can see and sense, deep within its bones, the beauty and humanness of it all
to be sure, there is much that heaviness can understand that lightness cannot - i can spill much ink about the virtues of heaviness and its importance too

but in the matters of being immersed in the rhythms of the beating heart, all must ground itself among lightness

https://t.co/CxN8TVsPrp

heaviness extorts us on a quest of Knowing, whether of the mechanics of the world or of the meaning of life

with lightness, through simply manifesting as it and living its ways, that god-sized hole within us begins to look more and more like a donut
and we can't help but laugh, and laugh, and laugh, reveling in the sorrowful glee that is living

For sure! Particular flavours of suffering can serve as a signal that your defence mechanisms are working properly. They're familiar, they work in keeping you safe. Resolving that suffering can feel like falling into a void where you don't know if it's joy or even worse pain https://t.co/dTBXjEj1AF

The "know" here is embodied, experiential, intuitive knowing, which is what most of our mechanisms work on and test safety by. 

A lot of our internal suffering comes for protecting ourselves from even worse suffering
The thing is, those tactics might be out of date, and if that's the only thing you knew how to do, for a lot of very good reasons it can be very hard to dissolve it and construct another
One tact might be to gently show yourself "the other side" (loving, warm) and gain your own trust that it is actually safe to let go / transmute

Another one, from my understanding of Bruce Tift, might be to stay with the suffering and to just sit with its experience without spinning stories around it, and imagining what you'd do if that suffering is here to stay for the rest of your life

That sounds ineffectual, but he has a whole set of logic around this which I'll try to crudely and probably inaccurately explain (I haven't finished the book yet)

Defence strategies come from trying to not feel intense experiences: shame, abandonment, but also good ones like joy
The thing is, we're always already feel them anyway - those mechanisms just subvert, suppress, turn our attention away from them. However, that doesn't stop those feelings from acting on us
So the move is to stick with the feelings and do our best not to judge it, not to relate those feelings to internal stories - just sit with it, even if it's incredibly uncomfortable
What this does is it deactivates the need for the defence mechanisms which served to help us stop feeling those feelings

A (to me) radical thing he proposes is - what if that pain will just be there forever? What if we're not trying to solve it in anyway? Because to try and solve it either through defence mechanisms or through better patterns is often to not be fully accepting of them

We then divide ourselves against ourselves, continually in conflict

Things that are suppressed will always show up again in some new disguise, you can't kill the feeling and not have it zombify in some new form (my thoughts around this is a bit more nuanced but anyways)

And so utter acceptance is the total base from which anything else ought to take place

And after this move of dissolvement and deconstruction and what he calls total kindness, I might guess that we then move towards constructing a new relationship with it

@christineist this is reminding me of tyson yunkaporta's excellent "Sand Talk", where he talks about how the australian aboriginals have a multitude of pronouns delineating relation, e.g I-myself, we-two, we-but-not-others, us-two, we-altogether

i think much of how my life currently unfolds could be thought of as "controlled accidents" - i can direct it towards some direction very intentionally, but whether or not that pans out is dependent on factors far beyond my control

so, i set myself up to be "accident prone"

this idea of "accident prone" has been a keystone in how i think because it's so broadly applicable

i got it from buddhist circles where they talk about meditation as not to definitively reach awakening, but rather to make it more likely to accidentally happen
the way i've understood it is that you can create situations, environments, conditions, parameters that can increase the likelihood of something happening

you can constrain the possibility space to some degree (minimize amount of different things, variance, that can happen), and increase chance (sharpen the probability curve so more % falls where you want it to), but there's always edge cases and unaccountable external influences

the phrase "luck = preparation + opportunity" is a good example 

preparation sets up the environment, opportunity is the 'accident'
one concrete example might be moving to a fast-paced city like NYC to "find success", or to LA to get into the movie industry - increases chances, but not definitive

another example was one i used in a paper thinking through what makes us experience something as "poetic" 

one point i argued is that things like rhyme, meter, metaphors - these are all tools that make experiencing something (e.g a poem) as poetic more "accident prone"
but even for the very acclaimed and popular pieces of poetry, art etc, not everyone likes them because in the end, it still depends on *resonance*
every interaction will involve the "will" (the controlled circumstance) and some other, out-of-your-control part of the world (whether that's your internal world or the external one)
this is an interaction that requires the input from *both sides*, and so it is the *in between* that determines the outcome
a last example i'll draw on is from christopher alexander's "the timeless way of building"

there are buildings and spaces that look totally different from one another, and yet they all feel whole and alive - what's up with that?

alexander says that it's because underneath them all they have the "quality without a name"

and this quality comes not because of some specific thing - some particular type of column or window - it's not the form (aesthetic, physical shapes) that matters

it is instead the *pattern of relationship* between the forms, elements, events that gives rise to that quality
and alexander abstracts it beyond just building too - it also relates to your habits, to group dynamics, to plants and so on
harmony comes from how things are in context to one another, how they relate - the wrong thing can be exactly the right thing in the right context

an example he gives is the courtyard and how a courtyard that is alive contains some kind of halfway space between indoors and outdoors (like a porch), and multiple paths leading in and out of it, and has a view out to a larger space
all of these point to how elements relate to one another to create an overall experience - having multiple paths into a private room would obviously make no sense, for example

also, how those paths look, how the porch looks, is of much less important than the activities that they allow and how they're placed in relation to one another

and in this way, from a single pattern, 10,000 forms arise

now you could think that you could take these patterns, apply them, and everything will be magically alive and have that quality, but from the very start alexander says that this 'method' of his cannot be applied mechanically, and he gives more examples later on in the book
even the relationship between these forms continuously change across multiple scales - these patterns are fluid, living, and ever-changing

it never becomes about fixating on the product but about closely attuning to this flowing process that sets up environments and conditions within which something you want may be "accident prone" to happen
and for me right now, that is the only way anything i move towards can happen, resting in that gap between order and chaos

meditation is sisyphean in both the sense that you can't get to the "end", but also that there is indeed a "goal", a "path", and a "direction" that you're heading towards, along with a continual burning desire (eros) to get to the top

i think that's Good and Useful actually https://t.co/AeCfKKxUGh

this is contra the "meditation isn't for anything, just be" folks, which is certainly another important & useful view that i've gotten much out of and i'm sure i will in the future too, but it's not end all be all

before you start fighting with me, don't (because i'll lose lmao) 

instead make your own thread and go argue with burbea in the two talks linked below 

the second one talks about desire and longing about half way through

https://t.co/2UBwlFJXlX
https://t.co/3Lvj9oVpei

some necessary extensions to the sisyphus image: 

the path & goals are non-linear and multiple, the boulder does continually roll down (and sometimes that's actually helpful), but in broad scheme of things not all the way (assuming no heavy psyche damage etc)

there are also landmarks along the way, e.g (depending on the path) jhanas, metta (and feeling the energy), insights etc

these are things you "get", "attain" etc - of course there's more and less skillful ways to relate to them, but to erase goals completely feels too blunt
the landscape also changes the more you go up, and eventually it becomes more about vibing "asymptotically" towards the goal (in mark lippmann language), grooving deeper and deeper

you'll always still be pushing the boulder because there's always more work to do (chop wood, carry water), but the nature of the boulder and the pushing shifts 

i'm certainly missing a lot of nuances, but i think broad picture it probably makes sense as a stance

on the sisyphus and eros point (this is getting into very shaky territory that i'm kinda just throwing out there without too much thought because it feels interesting but is probably quite off), drawing from the context below

https://t.co/GwhlAEnd2F

an unfolding of the self towards the unification with the awakened being, with awareness etc - the "work" as an overcoming of _lack_ (eros is lack, as socrates says)

on that same day of Beauty, i also felt happiness for the first time, something that was alien to even the furthest edges of my memory 

that euphoria 

https://t.co/TAsxElFTGs

i couldn't even figure out what it was until an hour in when — ah — this is what i had spent the past year actively and desperately searching for

i remember thinking that i had not a clue what people meant when they said they loved their friends

terrible emotional wrenching was certainly no stranger, so i definitely knew what it was to feel things! 

but i wasn't depressed & my childhood was mellow

yet, when i looked inside during the better moments of life, all i found was a spotless ivory wall
when that changed, one thing i came to realise is that the true claims of good love being removed from emotion are only from those who are able to fundamentally root their love in that felt sense

i'm not saying that every moment and action should have a warm fuzzy feeling for it to be love — that's ridiculous!

but rather without that root, how i relate remains stuck in the operations of the detached mind which follows coolly those understandable laws of ‘Loving Well’
that wellspring of sweetness was never available to me, but that embodied taste splashed fresh colours where there was not, and gave internal conviction that i, too, can become human

and i would joke that i'm a robot who's starting to feel things and it's terrible because now i get all emotional all the time and it's all so inconvenient

but pain only gains meaning in beauty and levity
god withdrew from the world so that the terror of being dashed against existence can jolt us into becoming transfixed by the glory of creation
and blessèd am the undeserving i, who now, at last, glimpses the divine hiding among those crinkles of your eyes

yet, they *also* still have a focus on spontaneity! but their conception would be different from how most of us would think of it

i'm not saying stimulus & environment is arbitrary though, it's all very hard especially if e.g you've got a soul sucking job that dominates your life

the more authentic i am in conversation, the more present i can be

but it's not always safe to be very authentic or very present because full presence involves taking the whole of the other in me and the giving of me to them

and also, bunnies

i've been thinking a lot about safety & what i'm protecting and why - there's various defences and ego things that serve a purpose but that i'd be better off untangling them and releasing, but there's also smol bunny parts that should not be bared to random ppl

letting someone hold your bunny is always a leap of faith and there's a great deal of trust involved in believing that someone can hold that bunny well, and it's a big responsibility when someone lets you hold theirs too

such an act can't be expected or demanded, ever

self-work is in part the work of creating swole bunnies whose inner strength broadens the scope of how much psychic turbulence can be handled, and often that doesn't mean just not getting hurt but being able to recover well from it

the task of "love" is in part the ability to both understand other people's bunnies well and to hold them in a safe way, and it sometimes also helps mitigate damage because you see that it's not a monster who's coming at you but a really hurt bunny

swole bunnies help me be much more authentic and present more often because there's less risk of being hurt in a deep or lasting way

in a good community and / or a tight-knit group of friends, a bunny can become swole "simply" through continously being held lovingly by others

and so it becomes this beautiful feedback loop - the wrinkle is if the bunny's sole source of strength is from the community only, or if it's able to internalise some of it

sole reliance on community (from 1 person (e.g your partner) to many) can be great until it becomes a cult

but also a great community is one of the most robust and wholesome ways to help nurture swole bunnies - having many independent deep connections that you can lean on & also have sharp discernment becomes, then, a key "skill" or "thing you ought to continuously be fostering"

i also find myself sometimes trying to shield the bunny so much that it's hard for others to be able to hold it and for it to strengthen in their love, and so learning to open myself & trusting them & loving them is all important too

i don't know where i was going with this

anyways

may your bunnies be loved, 
may you have swole bunnies

in the spirit of TOAD MODE i will riff out loud on today's Discourse:

stuff we feel points to something real and sacred

feelings can be directly altered by us

that can seem really unnatural and sometimes obscures the real thing but it can also change that thing for the better

i'm thinking it this way: 

there is a set of interlocking patterns at varying levels that is "us" - feelings exist at a layer, and they're intertwined with underlying an underlying layer

e.g anger (layer a) points to core wound (layer b)

layer b is the """"real"""" stuff (i dunno what this means), and sometimes that real stuff is sourced from something beyond us (some perception is not just from our generation but a direct result of channeling something else)

perception *is* simulated, though, and we can fuck around with it to a large degree, sober or otherwise - doing that sometimes doesn't untangle anything at the "real" layer though 

symptom / cause idea

but! repeated fucking around with perception e.g in insight experiences in meditation (non-dual, oneness, ecstatic jhanas), especially if repeated but sometimes someone just needs a single "hit", can permanently change a deep part of the underlying stuff

the problem with states is the fact that they're temporary and generated and unstable - they're "artificial" and runs against the "natural"

"natural" here meaning "baseline", our default state, what the majority of our underlying stuff is up to and generating

but here's the rub - what is "natural" for one person is very different for another, and we might think there's a generalised "human nature" but if we look at something like 'Preconquest Consciousness' that can differ to a far greater extent than we might be lead to believe

that's at the broad human potential level, but on the individual level that baseline can change a lot too - some modalities deal with it directly (IFS, EK, bio-emotive), taking feelings as a real "truth", a trusty "finger" pointing to moon & sometimes inseparable from the moon

and then there's the state way where (i think) it can expand what we think of as the moon - what we think is possible 

this stuff is sometimes in the "unknown unknown" territory - we don't know what the "there there" is like until we are actually there ourselves
but even if we get a taste of it once that isn't enough because it's still there and we're still here, and so repeated visits helps build a bridge and / or integrate the "there" into the "here"

problem is it's hard to tell when we're making stuff up that lies on shaky foundations or if we're actually expanding into something

chasing highs that only leave behind craving and no "trait" change is one good sign that it's not on solid ground

there might also feel to be some sort of violence done to the pre-existing "real" layer to just point outside of it in order to help it along, especially with states that are very clearly generated by us e.g jhanas (mystical experiences of oneness etc are less clear in this way)

but i don't feel that they're in opposition - those kinds of things are often done in a specialised container with the explicit goal to integrate back with the "real" stuff, and can sometimes in themselves help us be in a better place to work with the "real stuff"

there might be another worry that to shift away from our current "natural" patterns and baseline is unnatural

aside from the idea that there are multiple different "natural" patterns, how does this work with various buddhist claims of returning to most natural as the goal?

i don't like the "you're already enlightened" idea, i like my paths and my desire for transformation and for awakening 

the way that i understand that idea so i don't get angry every time i hear it is that all of us have this potential in us, this "original face"

but there's a bunch of stuff blocking us from it, or layers and layers of onion that hide the "true", "most natural" core

our current underlying patterns might actually be running a little wonky

by untangling, peeling back, releasing, like how the fist effortlessly unclasps, we get this more natural state that is different from our original starting point

in this way (zen ppl might claim), it is nothing special at all bc it was always there
there's analogies of us continuously tensing a muscle and that's all we knew and after some deep work has been done that tension is gone because it's been unclenched

for the schools of thought that i subscribe to, eventually it doesn't matter whether you're tensed or not because there's this meta-relationship to all of it that helps us flow with it - one possibility is the zen master who has forgotten that they were ever enlightened

so: the two approaches are that - two approaches, but they help one another. 

both require care in different ways, both i think require sensitive attunement, and a lot of us change a lot and so much of what we feel is "default" / "natural" can change too

AND there are ways to make it even more so through practices etc but this is so far out of the overton window of the average person

not that that's bad or anything it's just it's so much of who i am right now and is so important to me that i feel a pull to be like "but you don't get it!! you don't understand the brilliance that is possible and that i want to share with you!!!"
this is somehow even harder with religious people because i'm like i'm on your side!! i want you to experience god in all its glory and radiance!!!

this is, of course, how people become insufferable evangelists and i would become the exact thing i hate but even in the tweet below there's an assumption that there is some path to some end(s) and all of that is worthy of devoting your life to

https://t.co/pic5gr8P0L

i'd like to gently communicate the fullness of that without coming off half-insane and far too pushy in a tight and limited scope of a very time-bound non-tpot social context

it's important because i think to drench in beauty and the sacred will help enrich ppl's souls

and why that is important is for reasons that go beyond the scope of what i can articulate and i think if i can figure this out in a variety of ways (poetic or pragmatic or materialist or ...) that resonant with people it will be the bridge and invitation that i'm looking for

when i was doing open awareness meditation i remember asking my teacher if it made sense to "surrender" to the gap between thoughts, and really that whole practice requires a faith that awareness knows how to naturally unfold towards what's good https://t.co/KgpQ2jbAtx

currently in the process of reorienting to conversations and self-worth

before i went primarily idea first - i am an archive that i link together with things you're talking about & the degree to which i can do that well marks my worth

this served in place of a kind of faith

it feels good to do but it also becomes constrictive as i need to be Intelligent and Interesting

presence becomes somewhat irrelevant and i get stuck in my head

right now i feel like i know even less than i did a year or two ago which i don't really understand how that happened 

i don't know anything about design (my job), i don't have anything rich to share about my experiences with psychedelics or meditation

when people ask me what i'm interested in the best i can come up with is something like "the sacred, beauty, & reaching for god", which is nothing and everything - it's hard as an entry point bc it's not very concrete

i feel emptied out, a smooth bowl with no fruits, a freshly generated little guy

it's getting me to become attuned to other ways of orienting towards people and towards the conversation, which really is a living thing that is co-created

abundance is a big center - there is abundant time and social goodwill, we can take this slowly, there is much space to simply pause as we gather our thoughts and meander around things and find what we're excited to share with one another

awkwardness from not knowing how to connect is fine if both people can sit with it 

i was thinking about this the other day after going to a giant tech party how i'd much prefer to talk to any of those people on a 1:1 walk than meet them in that situation

attention flits too quickly and any distance felt is readily turned away from

in 1:1s, i'm forced to be with them and try uncover what's Really Real - time and attention bores in as i grasp for connection, a mutual excavation takes place

i mentioned that ideas replaced faith - it's bc ideas were always something i can fall back on, it's the closest medium to words and so more ideas = more things to talk about

having space then becomes a failure of ideas and a perceived failure of relation

but that's completely the wrong frame - ideas and conversation are the proxy we use to bridge the heart

i was using ideas as an effortful scaffolding to prop up the living medium & environment we co-create and share in relating

that's not necessarily a bad thing but i think i must first have a faith in our mutual ability to co-create and shape, to trust the spontaneous things that occur to me and to allow myself be guided by what we've made together

space and pause is active, it is potent, it is creation

it requires patience and a sensitivity to what emerges

my previous mode would rush because i felt like i needed to prove something and to do it as quickly as possible, which tinges everything with a desperation

i really like phone calls but one of its downfalls is not being able to watch the other person as they're processing something - it's really beautiful to be with

everyone has a unique expression in contemplation, eyes take on a different character, soften or crinkle

the space in contemplation is full and vibrant, not dead and silent

it can be so easily stomped over if i'm anxious to say My Thing or to present a certain self-image

but really, i think, warmth and presence is enough, and i can rest in the faith that there is a wellspring of vibrancy that's always right at the edge of awareness

the task then is to steadily watch, to channel, to flow with ease, with gentleness, with play

ideally i want to operate from a place of full abundance & gifting - i'm more than happy to just pay for stuff for friends esp if it's something small like coffee, but i hate owing people things generally and i also dislike fighting over the bill

lots of reasons for this [...] https://t.co/7bzjQoNoKB
the fact that splitting the bill is clean - emotionally that feels Good Actually - i *don't* want to be entangled with these people, i want to be independent as much as i can

a lot of this is definitely holdover from upbringing, but it's good default defense against bad actors - if you see the world as self-serving first (as my parents do), being extremely careful as to who you extend generosity to is the correct and safe move, it's how you survive

a lot of these past years have been trying to unlodge that out of my brain (in it tangled viewing people as fundamentally selfish + the way to win is to outcompete them) while still heeding the truth of it (i.e not be naive and let ppl take advantage of you)

the wonderful thing though is if i find friends that i like a lot (as i have recently), i simply want to give them things and so the part of me that gets stuck remembering debts turns a blind eye in these relationships

much more freeing and joyful that way - the heart sings

one key aspect of the state / trait distinction seems to be the difference between "the thing" and "the relation to the thing"

this is why, eventually, having thoughts or no thoughts, having a self or no-self are both a good time

i think the reason why trying to get rid of thoughts or transcend the self is the first doorway you see in a lot of these paths is bc some of the most bummer inducing ways we relate to things show up most strongly in how we relate to our thoughts and our self

it's actually clear from the core idea of "clinging" in buddhism - clinging can be thought of as an action and / or a way you relate to something

in that case both is true

where some get stuck though is they confuse the state that contains the relation to the thing with the relation to the thing itself, e.g:

"no self states is where i am not clinging, thus i should always be in no self"

but one of the purposes of those states is to give you a felt sense of what not clinging is like from a particular angle so you can start applying it everywhere else with anything

self and no self are equally empty

this is why i like the paths that move towards ~nondual end points bc they recognise that all of it is rich and can be a good time 

constricting it to certain states forever is a skill issue (to put it in a harsh and unnuanced way) bc it misses out on the core "relation"

and so the way that they might begin practice right off the bat is something like open awareness where you remain uninvolved but still let the thought run its course rather than returning to the breath or something

i'm not saying that way is the best approach for everyone just bc it's in some ways more direct to what might be the root of the issue - figuring out which practice works for you is a highly personal affair

but precisely keeping in mind the vast, colourful variety of practices that all seem to be "good" and "work" is a great way to unstick This Is The Truth type beliefs

perhaps "clinging / non-clinging" is only one of many root relations to uncover - maybe it's not even the root!

anyways, the interesting thing to me about east asian parental relationships in the context of the west (e.g 2nd gen immigrant like me, maybe particularly also ea guys) is this simultaneous push away for individual freedom and pull towards out of filial piety

as far as i can tell there isn't as much this baggage in west (seems like y'all have different ones / it's diff flavored) 

curious now of the typical relational patterns / archetypes in either clusters of cultures - individualistic vs collectivist & subtypes prob covers lots

i did this and didn't like music until I rediscovered it on my own and realised that my 10 years of classical musical education was worth basically nothing

two problems with OP's plan: 
1. kid resents it 
2. classical musical education is shit and makes you resent it

solutions: https://t.co/4DIjFnaQ0a

classical musicians used to be able to improvise

now they can't

this is the core symptom of a root issue

let's say on the slim chance that kid likes classical piano on their own - the kid then tries retain it throughout adulthood but learning a piece is such an arduous process everytime that at some point it no longer becomes fun

common sentiment might be "I've been playing for x number of years / decades and still feel like i don't understand Music - and I can't sightread" 

the path to properly build those fundamentals after all that time is very painful

ive rarely seen any asian kid post childhood retain their music practice, unless if they turned to some jamming form like jazz via joining school's big band or decided  pop / rock etc and form a band with friends

there is a way to do musical education properly but that requires a lot of foresight as to what to actually teach your kid from day 1

or you have a kid who REALLY likes music and fucks around and finds out on their own (ideal)
the solution to 1, or the way to maximize possibility of 1 not happening, is to actually really like music yourself as a parent

why are you trying to get your kid to play? is it for parental clout of your shiny kid or is it for the love of music and wanting to share that?

how you the approach encouraging your kid to play music will hugely influence how they relate to music themselves

I always think of László Polgár and his wife and their 3, very intentionally cultivated prodigious chess daughters

they managed to do that through having their daughters love it, not through tiger parenting it

how do you, as a parent, foster an environment where both you and your kid can relish in the joy of music? 

that is how you can "get" them to love music and retain that connection for a lifetime

shifting to general recent thoughts

utility of sight-reading is for: 

fun (read + play stuff i want quickly), 

for technical skills (be able to play certain kinds of hand positions / rhythms -> transfer to improv) which then helps 

learning stuff from other songs (figure out chords & patterns on keyboard)

learning improv has i think two main parts: 
1. transcribing what's in your head to your instrument (1 - ear training , 2 - technical ability)
2. having stuff you spontaneously hear in your head become richer (transcribing, very intentional listening, internalizing sounds etc)

a big part of ear training for me is to be able to distinguish sounds to begin with - how does the IV chord sound different from the V? what unique "taste" does each of them have?

if i can't even distinguish them then trying to compose with them is a lost cause

being able to do that is not trivial - i think i've listened to 500+ song snippets that have I, IV, V, vi chord in them and i still have some trouble getting it 100% correct - what really helped recently was playing around with them on the piano with LH with simple improv on RH

a song becomes more beautiful to me when i'm able to play it not in the sense that i can play it well, but that my attention is fully on it and its sounds - i just haven't been able to get that sort of attention when only listening

i've come to enjoy baroque stuff this way

i've been dwelling on how to deal with the heartbreak of transience, how to hold the brief sparks of friendship and how to honour the bitterness of letting go
i meet these beautiful people but they don't live nearby and are hard to stay in touch, or there are those that if only there were more space and time, maybe something deeper could've caught fire
i come to this city which is a place that at last feels like home, a place where i can rest, yet i don't know if i'll be forced out of this respite having barely just found it

i come to this country that brims with *my* people, yet the relationships and communities i've so cherished might become translucent at a moment's notice, locked in digital space at an agonizing distance that feels always just a step away but that can never be reached
i have dreams of the future and yet i cannot with any certainty tell you where i'll be in 3 months time - perhaps i'll be back to the country where i was born, seeking again, longing once more
god willing i'll still be soaking in the wonders of this vibrantly patchwork city, but i haven't much to lean on except for faith
"i'd love to see you when you visit SF!" i say, as if i comfortably dwell here, steadfast and ready to receive them for as long as they have a memory of me and this place of mine

all this uncertainty isn't really stressful or anxiety inducing, it's just i don't know if i'll "be around" in any real, meaningful sense, being able to hop on the train at a moment's notice to visit friends, residing in places where they can come visit me

it's clear to me now that i have to develop the capacity to savour every moment i get with the people whom i feel kinship with because otherwise i just leave them with a piece of my heart that will always yearn and cry for lost time

and it's not some flashy thing, it's really just about relishing it all — idle mornings, standing next to them while we silently watch the bloom of activity in front of us and taste the newness of each other's presence, the soft, mutual uncovering of our lives that long ride home

being with people i really click with is so easeful that i can forget how much of a miracle it is every single time it happens, and how getting to tend to it, together, over the hours and days and months is an irreplaceable joy
the worst part about loving someone is that they'll be gone one day and sometimes it makes me feel hopeless to try at all

but of course you must anyway because it is a greater tragedy to have never known them than to have being able to behold their beauty, to cherish them, and to grieve the inevitable loss, for this is the place where the soul sings and that is reason enough to give it our all

every time i've talked about something intellectual recently i later get this emotional whiplash and i think i gotta get back into vibe-maxxing bc trying to be Smart makes me wanna melt into the ground and shake and shake until i'm a puddle again

interesting exercise to think about what the best version of myself in a conversation / relationship looks like if i never said anything with intellectual content 

an answer i got back was soft radiance + really high warmth + groundedness + intimate attunement & curiosity

i have some kind of belief that i have to be useful to this person / not waste their time and the only way to do that is through ideas, or i need to show my Interestingness so that i'm cool and liked

totally not necessary! all of that is a side effect of The Thing

if i didn't have "lore" to share and "ideas" to relate, how do i still connect to this person in front of me in a meaningful way?

words are construction material to get to The Thing, the right few words at the right time (pointing out instructions) can immediately warp you there

self confidence can get misconstrued as egotistical superiority but the exact opposite is the case 

it requires a grounding that completely removes the person from games of comparison & competition - no longer need to feel superior as there's no insecurity of inferiority

some of the highest status people have an utter lack of interest in status! 

you can tell what their status is through how their deeper virtues show through + through how other people treat them

you can also go in the other direction and be the jester / fool who, due to being the lowest status becomes the highest status, which is the most interesting dynamic - would be curious to see examples of how this works in normal relationships

i can't stand a lot of lofi hip hop because in most songs it's like the same bar or two of vocal chop again and again and again and that drives me utterly nuts 

but! i recently connected the dots that it's bc it has its origin in hip hop beats which were repetitive on purpose

most hip hop beats originally didn't have a bunch of variation and just looped because you were supposed to rap over it - the beat purely served support to the rhyme which drove the track, not as its own melody

(lots of variation nowadays so it's def not a "rule")
and now you extract that into just instrumentals and make it ~ c h i l l ~ it becomes study music where *your thoughts* are the "melody", hence why it works so well as background music!

you can still have a sick chop though which i don't think most lofi music has, e.g jay electronica's exhibit c is bonkers, many of Count Bass D's in Dwight Spitz, MF DOOM of course (some of my favs are One Beer, Rap Snitch Knishes & Raid (which samples Bill Evans!!))

stream 001: when i feel fully satiated i have no desire to do anything else but to continue doing the thing - not checking my phone becomes natural rather than a result of breaking a "habit", which means too much screen time comes from trying to fill idle time in a lacking way

figure out the range of things i can do that feels really good but is "downtempo" *in place of* screens, rather than restricting screens itself

be pulled towards something rather than pushing myself away from something

stream 002: there are social situations that somehow, magically, make me feel social when i wasn't before. has only happened in workshop / retreat setting and this past week hanging out with coworkers for 8+ hours a day, 5 days straight

recovering from being social isn't true as a blanket statement anymore - i did have to recover for 2 full days after being jolted out of that context, but i think i could've kept on going for much longer, with much less "me time" than i thought i needed

also we didn't have "deep conversations", it was just really pleasant, normal conversation, joking around, trading small anecdotes

so much is uncovered already and so much to love - small mannerisms, the shape of them taking clearer form - looking forward to spending more time
getting alone time after dinner wasn't needed, nor did it feel isolating. it was just what it was, no big deal. could've kept on socialising and doing stuff together though

stream 003: what is going on for me when i feel i need to "recharge"?

before I thought it was to be by myself, and although it could involve that doing stuff that has a clear objective with friends (fetch desk, put together thing) fits that too

being around people in a gathering where objective is conversation vs being around people where objective is [some material goal], and conversation is incidental / side effect, feels very different and impacts my "energy level" in different directions

might have to do with expectations around how I ought to show up and *what part of me* should show up

actively needing to try & connect & do thinky stuff is a lot harder to be natural and spacious with on the fly than doing stuff together where i can just exist and do the thing

stream 004: there is tedious robot work and then there is soothing robot work

the difference is one requires just enough brain power to be annoying and not enough stimulation to be interesting, whereas the other one can be pure flow 

both, however, can be enjoyed with music

4 kinds of activities: 

1. manic: requires forward motion and drains energy

2. depressive: retreating but slow burns into a dull depressive state

3. energizing: forward but generates more energy than it expends

4. rejuvenating: retreating & recovers energy https://t.co/DDJHXYVFfL

i don't think i've found a forward + recover one yet that can sustain itself forever - i've experienced some steady-states but eventually requires "off time"

i think for "extroverts", socializing is forward + recovers while being alone is retreating + draining, and vice versa
i should note that just because something is draining doesn't mean it's not enjoyable - it just means you need time to recover after a period of time!
@_inju because it is made in harmony with the world and its spirits rather than trying to mimic it or overcome it

meditation log 001: got a small sense of unifying sounds into a single field of "sound" rather than having one particular sound (only the voice, only the cars driving by etc) dominating

it's a move of unblocking than expanding

and so with sounds it's leaning all the way back and not being automatically zoomed into just one

leads to interesting experience of everything being heard with equal importance - one whole field with peaks and valleys within

i'm really frustrated by my mum's "hardened by Real Life" view that you can't be a genuinely kind-hearted and generous person to anybody except for your family because "human nature" 

flares up a primal anger that makes me want to punch a tree
i genuinely think that despite her being christian she'd say that to actually strive to be like jesus is a completely naive, misguided and foolish endeavor

i think part of it is that it's flying in the face of my lived experience right now, of my morals and the type of person i want to become, of how i want to treat others, of the future that i want to build with my friends

there's a cynicism that i'm infuriated by

there's being a good, honest and generous person, and then there's imo the real problem of a lack of good discernment and boundaries

which, tbf is really hard and i can't exactly blame my mum for coming to this view that she has over the years given the circumstances

a pattern that she's pinpointed is:

person A gives kindness to person B -> B is initially grateful and then gets used to it, doesn't reciprocate (because they never do) -> any act from A that falters from gold standard leads to resentment in B (even if they're a good person!)

she said today that "it's exhausting to be kind to everyone all the time" and that feels to me to point to a central problem here that resulted in all of this

there are wise and naive ways to be kind-hearted

to be kind to others to the detriment of yourself feels like it follows a totalic and simplistic idea of what kindness means 

i have some intuition that it's a situation where the opposite is true of there being a real selfishness (or something?) in being that way
how do i square christian ideals of self-sacrifice (which i genuinely do believe to be a beautiful thing) with what i just said above? no idea lol

it's not this, but similarly flavored is how people pleasers are actually trying to get the other person to validate their Goodness and Worth and as a result place the burden of managing their own emotions onto their guests
there's a real difference between being really grounded in yourself and so people naturally feel comfortable around you / your presence vs constant micro-nervous accommodation and caretaking

there does feel to be some real dynamic to being nice to someone, them getting used to it, and despite them being a good person stuff gets twisted and now they're *expecting* that you'll e.g give them money

but i feel like this might come from 1. *your* expectation that being kind to them will lead them to be kind to you and reciprocate (and perhaps that's why you're kind at all) and / or 2. you feel *obligated* to be kind to people rather than it being a thing that's genuine to you

i think it's absolutely fair to be genuinely kind and want (and even expect!) that people will be kind back and be heartbroken when they're not and become very jaded when that happens (which is what happened to my mum)

there's a real "what's the point" + "i must protect myself"

i think the problem for me is that she conflates all of those into one big bundle and points the source of the problem to kindness rather than being able to notice when you're feeling obligated and that you're giving too much
there's also a thing of doing all of these things for people when they haven't asked for it and *expecting* them to notice and know the full weight of what you've given

how do you be kind while not being stepped on?

e.g, assuming that i'm doing a favor for a person whom i don't think understands the extent to which this was a hard task / favor to do for them, how do i communicate this without being transactional

i think this is the wrong frame and doesn't separate two kinds of favors - one i do for my friends and i'm just genuinely happy to do it for them and don't expect anything in return, the other *is* transactional

but i think there is something real of spending a bunch of effort and through no one's fault the receiver just does not know the amount of effort put in

how do you communicate, and *why* do you communicate it?

i think i'm also tangling in something of - if i communicate this, they will think that i'm doing it so to set an expectation of something in return

what do

ok moving on from that open question, which is one that does assume the other person is also a good person and would really care given good communication,

there's people who would take advantage of my kindness, and for those people i'd firmly distance myself

there's no less kindness and warmth, and this is assuming attempts of clear & good communication has happened, but this is where skillful boundaries come into play

so for "i must protect myself", it never has to come to such a point where you abandon kindness for safety

as for "what's the point", why i try to be kind-hearted is because the world around me appreciably darkens and becomes more vicious when i'm not striving for it, and more importantly it's a sacred thing in itself and that's all the reason i need

bahiya is actually one of my favourite stories, heard it first related in one of the kenneth folk + michael taft podcast eps

The days are passing again. 

Sometimes they're distinct with events that mark its discrete passage, but now it's resumed its undefined, imperceptible evolution

I looked back at what I've journalled these past few months, and the lack of record makes it feel as though they never happened at all

I sometimes worry about this - to forgo capturing is not only to lose it but to have it blink from any past and future somethingness

But what is there to say? 

What is there to say about how a great redwood changes when a handful of slipping seconds is all you have with it?
It seems that I'm becoming a more relaxed person. It seems that I've been feeling more gratitude and more love. These are wonderful things, but what is there to report about such simple beauty?

I remember hearing of a Zen poet who, upon his awakening, completely dropped off writing anything at all, for how the world just _is_ expressed everything he could ever hope to say and more

He eventually started writing again, and I guess I can't help but write
I went to LA for the first time this week. It's a strange place - an uncountable sprawl of corners and buildings and freeways. I don't think I really understand it

I don't like downtown (although it's less psychic-ly aggressive than SF's). Santa Monica's OK, though its tall palm trees in its suburban streets felt archetypally Californian

Pasadena - now that's somewhere that feels close to heart

I don't know what it is about the quiet suburbia. Maybe it makes me feel safe. Maybe it feels like a place that I can be for a long time, that I can really _settle_ into. Establish a rhythm. Know the local shops, the neighbours ...
weave this sweet texture that when I look back to it on my deathbed, I might not have much to say except that it was beautifully mundane, that it was a place where I could watch the redwoods grow

people structure themselves around certain patterns of ideas for motivations and reasons (their "why") that operate at a different layer from the ideas themselves - this is often what i'm especially curious about so i'm figuring out how to inquire into it well

me talking ideas in a way that feels good has the ideas be in direct service of being fodder for hitting at that different layer, e.g i bring up some person's notion of home as created from a repeated space of comfort so you to have more things to riff / reflect off of

conversations that i really enjoy are still mysterious to me though because it's flow (i.e not to do with the specific contents) - even the pauses are flow, and besides the pauses easily gets filled in by all the participants at some point anyway

i think "natural" is key - it doesn't feel strained

i can be asking a lot of questions and just listening and it feel fantastic vs straining to ask questions and figure out how to interact with this person

there can also be a seeming back and forth going on but the person's questions just don't feel good to me - not even that they're not interesting questions but that it just doesn't feel attuned or "real" or something, like we're going through the motions of """relating"""
oh hm interestingly enough i think it's in those strained conversations that i feel more keenly like i might talk too much despite often first trying to ask a lot of questions

there's a give (ask) / take (respond / talk) dynamic where i try to give at first but because of a lack of response i end up taking, on one hand in order to compensate but on the other in order to have more material / cards on the table for us to look at together
wonder if in failure modes there's a dynamic of the things i'm bringing up are not bringing up more things in you and vice versa

in a balanced give / take dynamic i'm not afraid of taking up too much space because i know the other person is comfortable taking too and have their monologues while i eagerly listen and absorb, and so i don't feel like i'm imposing as it's not one sided

both people are actually interested in what's being given and as more things are being said more stuff is laid out on the table to be played with together

so it's actually very exciting to listen to the other person talk AND be the one talking bc both contributes to the space

when you hit on a topic that's resonant for everyone involved, for me there becomes an excitement to relate experiences / ideas because they're now charged with a meaningful, alive context that assumes that they'll be responded to / played with
sharing something fundamentally becomes about "us" rather than "me"

even in a dynamic of e.g everyone's troubleshooting my relationship problem, even though i'm talking about my experience and they are giving ideas / experiences that are diagnostic rather than "pure share", both parties are still interested in what they're hearing and saying

and there's a feedback loop between the two rather than one sided lecture style, "let me tell you about this new idea" and the other person not really being able to relate to it / not really knowing what to do with it

again problem isn't content but about resonance of content

you can constantly be operating on level of ideas and still have a wonderful convo because both of you are implicitly connecting to these ideas at that deeper level, so talking more about it is bringing both into a richer relationship with it via your own reasons
e.g you can have a great long conversation about very technical and abstract buddhist concepts and it go on for 5 hours and be amazing, but

that's because both of you already deeply care about that, maybe one cares about it because they think it's one of many beautiful systems and thus inherently interesting, and the other bc it's a direct embodied source of meaning / coherence that is becoming more clarified

there are idea conversations where despite being really interested it becomes one way due to my lack of knowledge and that feels bad as a convo bc i'm not able to meaningful contribute something to the conversational playing ground

something subtle here, can't pin point yet
i'm running on this assumption that vast majority of people really care about the layer-underneath-ideas stuff so figuring out how to surface it well seems critical to a good conversation for me

i'm also realizing that everyone i enjoy talking to very actively just talks and expands on stuff without me needing to ask a question at every step, so frequently what they say in "a single breath" brings up 3 new directions to go in

i don't feel like i'm constantly trying to "pull" something out of them which really doesn't feel good, or i'm becoming an interviewer trying to get more facts out of them bc the things they've said don't create more deeper-layer-shaped handles to grab

that being said i think someone really skilled at relating to people can readily see such handles or create them in ways that i can't yet - stock fact only response can be an immediate jumping off point to something richer via just one well formed question / reflection

rn the ppl i like talking to have a willingness to share that more obviously exposes such entry points / handles, and so it's just easy to keep delving and exploring 

silence is comfortable because we both know each other's deeper-layer-thing

so there's a few threads above:

talking about ideas often feels bad to me -> when it does feel good it's bc there's understanding of deeper "why" /  the ideas that are brought up is in service of deeper "why"

we can also be talking about the "deeper why" questions directly, e.g what is home to you, why are you curious about x, how do you relate to god

these kinds of q hit on experiences / myths / the human condition etc that many / most people have a resonant relationship with

the specific content of the ideas doesn't actually matter, that i feel connected to the ideas from that "why" place does, *AND* that i'm able to meaningful contribute things into the conversational playground for both of us to play with

a good conversation is when we're saying things that places ideas / moods / intrigues etc into the playground that both of us are genuinely interested in, i.e that both of us feel like connects to our deeper why

because of this, both parties can riff off of one another well

and because of that, me saying something no longer becomes about only me, but becomes about us - about placing more things into the playground so that both of us can have an even better time

bc of this, the specifics of how much one person is giving or taking doesn't matter!

when conversations flow for me, it's because everyone in the convo is contributing to the playground and playing with the things in it together - what this has looked like fpr me are people who naturally share & expand on both ideas and how they're feeling about it etc

in doing this there's rich resonant things that are just brought up without having to "dig" for it 

good sharing naturally elicits (almost begs for) good responses

however! people who are really socially adept at this stuff need much less material initially to work with to kickstart the playground - they can figure out what's resonant for both parties involved very quickly

"good reply game" is exactly what they're doing, but constantly

this can be learned! my current thesis is that it has to start with genuine deep curiosity about the other person as a whole human being, and then lead from there

curiosity is a form of resonance, and so if the other person is fascinating to me, they can spend the whole night talking and I will still have a great time because it's just constantly resonant and interesting for me - my "contribution" here are my questions / replies

ah ok so this thing here i think is when the technicality / depth of ideas actually exceed the window of resonance - it becomes too in-the-weeds for me to grasp - although the broad category is resonant & it'll eventually be resonant, it's not right now

https://t.co/P0UOi7nxWU

and so it eventually runs out of steam because i'm not able to reply well, thus i can't even "contribute" good questions to the playground

at this point there's a skill of transitioning into something else or picking up prior thread & taking it back to something that's resonant

ah also i didn't talk about this but a really important factor is emotional stuff around being able to be attuned, playful, fluid etc & being able to have the other person feel safe - you shouldn't force intimacy!

it's provoking a lot more thoughts, threading below a meander through love, eros, attachment, craving & satiation, games & agency https://t.co/fjz6xbm43A

to start: there's a beautiful lecture by Pageau on love, specifically through the lens of Dante 

from The Divine Comedy: "Love alone is the true seed of every merit in you, and all acts for which you must atone"

those in hell are there because of *disproportionate love* - Dante thought that all things in the world are a kind of Good, but each exists on a hierarchy of Goods and each has a level that is proper for them to be loved at

the example Pageau gives is that of alcohol - alcohol is a Good to e.g serve as social lubricant, and set in its appropriate place can be used to serve the higher good of forming deeper connections with friends
(worth noting here the notion of "rationality" as rooted in "ratio", i.e proper proportion, rather than the more common sense idea of it as logic)

sympopp's "liking" and "craving" could be said to be two different structures / patterns / dynamics of love, but both are driven by this love towards the Good, and so there's something "true" about both of them

each kind of Good satiates us a different amount, but (I think) there is a real satiation of sort there, even if it doesn't ultimately satiate the need that will be long-term fulfilling but instead fulfills only some parts or masks that need temporarily

i don't feel it quite captures the dynamic to say that things we crave for don't satisfy us - it's more 1) what it's satisfying and 2) to what degree it satisfies

satisfying via "craving" is temporary, satisfying via "liking" (and resulting in pleasure) is more deeply fulfilling
i'm reminded of the idea of sin as "missing the mark", so here it might point to a disordered hierarchy of Good which entails a disproportionate love on some Good

i'd then want to avoid sinning not because it's Morally Bad but rather sinning just makes things suck a lot more 

but if this is the case, why go for the lower goods at all?

i have two buckets in mind:

1) liking the right thing in the wrong context
2) liking the right thing but through an unsatisfying way

put in another way: 

stimulating what's best suited to the situation with the right amount of love is what leads to a satisfying "pleasure"

but wanting something out of it that it cannot fulfill or in an unsustainable way makes it "craving"

to start with 1)

vervaeke talks about "having" vs "being" needs, where both are important but problems come when the two are confused, e.g "having" sex (consumption) in order to "be" in love (transformation)

hefner, the creator of playboy, has a different deal going on but same principles apply where his fixation on sex never helped him heal into what he wanted to "be"

https://t.co/c5oVaS7vg3
moving on, there's this thing called a "maieutic end", which are sort of "midwife" goals that you discard once you've reached it
e.g the goal of falling in love is not just to have somebody to love, but to (as c thi nyugen puts it) "take on their well-being as an intrinsically valuable, final end"
nyugen goes on to say that if falling in love were some final end, if his partner gets sick with an incurable cancer, that maieutic end would have him switch his love to somebody healthier ASAP

perpetual chasing of what's in a way intermediary goals of things like money, power, fame can at times be thought of as getting stuck on something that's meant to be disposed for some other ultimate goal

what this doesn't capture is that often there's ways to simply not take on goals of this nature - being truly valued among a small community you genuinely love and in which you have real impact probably hits at the same things that some who chase fame are actually shooting for
with 2), i want to consider: why is any particular hyperstimulus not universal? some people are completely disinterested in video games, doritos etc
i think it's because people's god-shaped-holes are very specifically them-shaped - we have different vices because of our unique patterns and needs

this actually puts greater weight on the claim of Christianity - that to orient towards God is to align all the Goods such that you *can* fulfill every such need in a way that is both deeply and universally satiating
to return to the patterns, this also includes things like attachment mechanisms and IFS type ideas where every one of these "copes" are adaptive behaviours / habits that *actually make sense* because
they were the best way we could reach towards that genuinely, deeply satiating Good given the malformed contexts that we were in

but best adaptions in malformed contexts can never reach true satiation because the context fundamentally blocks it, and continuing to operate by those patterns in new contexts starts shaping the contexts towards those old ones

(lots of nuance & caveats here but broad strokes)

i can't talk about craving without talking about jhanas, so briefly, they're interesting to me because they're a way of getting a specific kind of sensorial desire met (pleasure, joy) in a consistent and abundant way
i think sometimes there's a chase after something mainly because there's actually not enough of it - there's a real deprivation there

jhanas propose: what if we had some psychological equivalents of thirst for water but in the form of sensorial pleasure etc - getting an abundant amount of it anytime we want it would drop that sort of "craving" relationship with it because we can just get it on will

people move on from jhanas because that need actually becomes satisfied, and so the "hyperstimulation" of sensorial pleasure is actually not inherent to the pleasure itself but to the dynamics surrounding it

to return to love, another frame is to consider that craving isn't actually for the thing itself but for the *dynamics* of craving - we crave because we like that burning desire, and the object of desire is in some sense secondary to the relationship between you and that object
i'd need to think more on what exactly makes this chase, this tumultuous churning so linked with a sort of aliveness, but a lot of us really do like yearning
anne carson wrote on how eros is a triangle between the "lover, beloved, and that which come between them"

there is a delicious paradoxical where the thing that blocks the lover is at once that which separates the lover to the beloved *and also* that which connects (via making the eros - the lack, the yearning - possible)

interestingly christian literature is dotted with the eros for God, which really highlights that eros, while showing up most often in the context of romantic love, can be peeled back more to the dynamic of separation from and reaching for that most profound oneness and moreness

there's perhaps a fear of annihilation in such cases, of being subsumed by what we actually desire, and so the chase and distance is set up to keep the game going so that we can indulge in small tastes and glimpses but never truly face the immensity of our Want
actually this reminds me that pagaeu noted that true love is indeed to be subsumed by and in total unity with what we love but *at the same time* to be existing completely separate from it
the Trinity give themselves completely to one another while still remaining wholly separate, hence they are at once One and Three

uh abrupt transition but video games: i think the problem isn't so much that they're hyperstimuli and satisfy craving desires but that the ways that you are (the way that you are an agent) and the things you accomplish in the games are often not transferable to broader contexts

again, there is "real" accomplishment in there given the very simplified version of you fitted to the hyper curated environment, just as being really good at fishing or baking are "real" accomplishments (specific set of skills mapped to specific domains)
but fishing / baking tends to be set in broader contexts with a greater range of stuff you have to learn and deal with that are genuinely satiating or can be applied to other things that are so
but you can have video games that in playing them and completing them (and even with them having addictive, can't-put-down qualities to them) that have real impact

e.g visa has tweeted about god of war a bunch, or a better example would be a friend of mine tattooing Dark Souls because it taught him determination and agency in a way that very much changed how he was in the real world
ok that's all the thoughts, there's still some things that feel unresolved to me but i'll leave it here for now
